UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile on the AlgoTube platform, storing their personal information, preferences, and social connections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: Unique username chosen by the user, used for display across the platform.

email.description: User's email address for contact and account recovery. (Not for authentication)

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture or avatar.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

interests.description: A list of tags or keywords representing the user's interests, used by the recommendation engine.

joinedAt.description: Timestamp when the user registered on the platform.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

subscribedChannelIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs of channels this user has subscribed to. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

subscriberIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs of users who have subscribed to this user's channel. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video uploaded to the AlgoTube platform, including its metadata and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the video content.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the video's thumbnail image.

videoUrl.description: URL to the video file stored in Firebase Storage.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the video was uploaded.

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the video in seconds.

viewsCount.description: The total number of times the video has been viewed.

uploaderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who uploaded this video. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Video)

tags.description: A list of keywords or tags associated with the video, used for search and recommendations.

category.description: The primary category or genre of the video (e.g., 'Tech', 'Gaming', 'Music').

likesCount.description: The total number of likes the video has received.

dislikesCount.description: The total number of dislikes the video has received.

commentsCount.description: The total number of comments on the video. (Aggregate for display)

processingStatus.description: The current processing status of the video (e.g., 'uploaded', 'processing', 'ready', 'failed'). Updated by Cloud Functions.

searchIndexTerms.description: Extracted keywords and metadata used to build the Trie (Prefix Tree) for type-ahead search suggestions.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a user's comment on a video, including replies to other comments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

videoId.description: Reference to the Video ID this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Video 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who posted this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was posted.

parentCommentId.description: Optional reference to the ID of the parent comment, indicating this is a reply. (Relationship: Comment 1:N Comment)

likesCount.description: The number of likes this comment has received.

dislikesCount.description: The number of dislikes this comment has received.

UserVideoInteraction.title: UserVideoInteraction
UserVideoInteraction.description: Tracks specific interactions a user has with a video, crucial for the recommendation engine and analytics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserVideoInteraction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user involved in the interaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserVideoInteraction)

videoId.description: Reference to the Video ID that was interacted with. (Relationship: Video 1:N UserVideoInteraction)

watchDurationSeconds.description: Optional: The duration in seconds the user watched the video during this interaction. Relevant for 'view' or 'watched_partial' types.

percentageWatched.description: Optional: The percentage of the video watched by the user during this interaction (0-100). Relevant for 'view' or 'watched_partial' types.

tagsAtInteraction.description: Optional: A snapshot of relevant video tags or user interests at the time of interaction, used for the recommendation engine.

Playlist.title: Playlist
Playlist.description: Represents a collection of videos curated by a user, which can be public or private.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Playlist entity.

description.description: A description of the playlist content.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who owns this playlist. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Playlist)

videoIds.description: References to Video IDs included in this playlist. (Relationship: Video N:N Playlist)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the playlist was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the playlist was last updated.

isPublic.description: Indicates if the playlist is publicly viewable (true) or private (false).

definition.description: Primary storage for user profiles, uniquely identified by Firebase Auth UID. Secure by path-based ownership, where '{userId}' matches 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores all publicly viewable videos. Includes denormalized 'uploaderId' field for authorization independence, allowing owner-only write operations while being publicly readable. Supports global listing and search queries.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier of the video.

definition.description: Stores comments associated with a specific video. Includes denormalized 'userId' (of the commenter) for authorization independence, allowing owner-only write operations while being publicly readable.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent video.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

definition.description: Tracks private interactions of a specific user with videos (e.g., views, likes, shares). Secured by path-based ownership, with '{userId}' matching 'request.auth.uid'. The document also contains 'userId' for data consistency.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user performing the interaction, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: interactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user video interaction record.

definition.description: Dedicated top-level collection for playlists marked as 'isPublic: true'. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, allowing owner-only write operations while being publicly readable. Enables efficient global listing of public playlists.

params.name: playlistId
params.description: The unique identifier of the public playlist.

definition.description: Stores private playlists belonging to a specific user (where 'isPublic: false'). Secured by path-based ownership, with '{userId}' matching 'request.auth.uid'. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for explicit rule clarity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the playlist owner, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: playlistId
params.description: The unique identifier of the private playlist.

firestore.reasoning:
The AlgoTube Firestore structure is designed for security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **UserProfile (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** User profiles are uniquely identified by their Firebase `auth.uid`. The `userId` wildcard in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, establishing clear, path-based ownership. No additional `get()` operations are required for authorizing access to a user's own profile, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rules. The `UserProfile` document itself is the authorization context.
2.  **Video (`/videos/{videoId}`):** Each `Video` document includes a denormalized `uploaderId` field. This allows security rules to directly check `resource.data.uploaderId == request.auth.uid` for write operations (create, update, delete) without needing to fetch any parent document. Read access is public.
3.  **Comment (`/videos/{videoId}/comments/{commentId}`):** Each `Comment` document includes a denormalized `userId` field, representing the commenter. This enables direct ownership checks (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) for modifying a comment, eliminating `get()` calls. Read access is public.
4.  **UserVideoInteraction (`/userProfiles/{userId}/videoInteractions/{interactionId}`):** These interactions are private to the user. The `userId` in the path segment immediately secures these documents by linking them to `request.auth.uid`. The `UserVideoInteraction` document also includes `userId` for data integrity and explicit context, but authorization is primarily path-based.
5.  **Playlist (Segregated):** Playlists are segregated into two collections based on their privacy: 
    *   **Public Playlists (`/playlists_public/{playlistId}`):** Each document includes a denormalized `ownerId`. This allows security rules to check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` for write operations without `get()`. Read access is public.
    *   **Private Playlists (`/userProfiles/{userId}/playlists_private/{playlistId}`):** These are secured by the `userId` in the path, ensuring only the owning user can access them. The `ownerId` is also denormalized within the document for explicit context, allowing direct `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` checks.

This extensive denormalization and path-based ownership strategy ensures that authorization decisions are always self-contained within the document being accessed or its immediate path, completely eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules. This makes rules atomic, performant, and significantly easier to debug.

**Support for QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns):**

1.  **Structural Segregation:** The design strategically segregates data based on its security posture:
    *   **Public Read, Owner Write:** `/videos/{videoId}` and `/playlists_public/{playlistId}`. These top-level collections enable efficient global `list` queries for public content (e.g., trending videos, search results, public playlists) because all documents within them share the same public read access requirements. Rules don't need to filter results; they simply grant public read access to the entire collection.
    *   **Owner Read/Write (Path-Based):** `/userProfiles/{userId}`, `/userProfiles/{userId}/videoInteractions/{interactionId}`, and `/userProfiles/{userId}/playlists_private/{playlistId}`. These collections inherently support QAPs for user-specific data. A user can efficiently `list` their own profiles, interactions, or private playlists because the `userId` in the path segment ensures all documents within that subcollection belong to `request.auth.uid`. The rules simply enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all operations within these paths, making queries secure and efficient without requiring rule-based filtering.
    *   **Public Read, Commenter Write:** `/videos/{videoId}/comments/{commentId}`. This subcollection allows efficient listing of comments for a specific video, as all documents are publicly readable. Write access is restricted to the `userId` denormalized within each comment.

By carefully segregating data into collections with homogeneous security postures and denormalizing authorization-critical fields, the design ensures that Firestore Security Rules act as robust gates rather than filters, allowing for secure and efficient `list` operations without compromising performance or introducing complexity.
